title: quercus faginea var. pedunculata
intro: Quercus faginea var. pedunculata, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Quercus faginea var. pedunculata include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Quercus faginea var. pedunculata, such as infusions and decoctions, are often used to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of fever and infection.
